headline: 27% target achieved; strong teacher engagement masks uneven regional coverage and scaling readiness gaps.
collection_status: 365 of 1,350 responses collected (27% of target). Data quality is solid with 10 of 10 regions represented. Current trajectory suggests 730–850 responses by survey close if pace holds; significant acceleration needed to reach target.
key_findings: Teacher voices dominate (190 of 365; 52%): KiuFunza lower-primary teachers are primary respondents, validating classroom-level insights but limiting district/regional leadership perspective.
Expansion appetite is clear: 126 respondents (35%) prioritize KiuFunza expansion; policy integration (81) and new programs (80) signal readiness to scale if systems support it.
Critical scaling gap: average scale readiness is 2.7/5—stakeholders want growth but perceive weak infrastructure, digital tracking, and government coordination to enable it.

Geographic concentration risk: Lindi (70), Mara (64), and Tanga (52) account for 51% of responses; Rukwa (5), Singida (17), and Songwe (22) are severely underrepresented, limiting rural and remote insights.
flags: Scale readiness (2.7/5) is half partner satisfaction (4.4/5)—confidence in KiuFunza does not translate to belief in ability to grow it; investigate blockers in policy, staffing, and monitoring.

Regional imbalance threatens representativeness: Rukwa and Singida are critically low; Songwe and Shinyanga lag; without targeted pushes in these zones, insights will skew toward Lindi/Mara/Tanga ecosystems.
District/regional leadership undersampled: only 17 DEO, ARC, REO, and district-official respondents combined; survey risks missing critical system-level constraints on scaling.
recommendation: Immediately launch targeted collection in Rukwa (5→30), Singida (17→40), and Songwe (22→35) to achieve geographic balance. Simultaneously, deploy district/regional officer intake campaign (target +25 DEO/REO/ARC responses) to surface policy and infrastructure barriers blocking the 2.7 scale-readiness score.
